target.title: fix #90850: iMessage send can wedge on long-lived RPC; add one-shot send-rich fallback and receive-side troubleshooting
target.body:
## Summary

- Add guarded recovery for iMessage RPC text sends that time out after dispatch.
- Recover already-persisted outbound rows before considering any fallback send.
- Limit one-shot `imsg send-rich` fallback to RPC clients owned by the current send call, after the owned client is stopped, and keep caller-owned long-lived clients from resending.
- Add receive-side troubleshooting for the case where inbound iMessages never reach local `chat.db`.

Fixes #90850

## Maintainer readiness

- **Fix classification:** Root-cause bug fix for iMessage message-delivery timeout recovery.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** 86/100. The code path, fallback gates, docs, lint, typecheck, and focused tests are covered locally; the remaining gap is live macOS Messages delivery, which is not available from this Linux environment.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** When the long-lived iMessage RPC sender wedges, agent replies can be generated but never reach Messages. Users then see a configured and apparently healthy iMessage channel while outbound replies silently fail or cannot be correlated back to the sent GUID.
- **Scope boundary:** This only changes iMessage text-send timeout recovery and the iMessage troubleshooting docs. It does not change media sends, channel routing, gateway lifecycle, account configuration, receive implementation, or non-iMessage channels.
- **What did NOT change:** Media sends, channel routing, gateway lifecycle, account configuration, receive implementation, non-iMessage channels, and successful RPC send behavior are not changed; this PR only narrows the timeout recovery boundary for text sends.
- **Source-of-truth boundary:** `chat.db` remains the source of truth for proving an outbound send already landed. The patch does not introduce a new message schema, config key, provider protocol, or persistence format.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Public contract surface is unchanged: no schema, config, protocol, migration, or default setting changes. The canonical confirmation source remains `chat.db`; the related open PR scan found no same-contract or canonical PR; Apple Push and macOS Messages sync repair are out of scope.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** Existing successful RPC sends keep the same path. Timeout fallback requires a sent-row check first; one-shot `send-rich` is only attempted for a client created by the current call after that owned client is stopped. Caller-owned RPC clients keep the previous no-resend behavior on ambiguous timeouts.
- **Related open PR scan:** daily-fix linked-PR and public-contract scans found no related open PR for this issue or the detected `send.rich` / `chat.db` contract terms.
- **Out-of-scope boundary:** This does not attempt to repair Apple Push, macOS Messages sync, SIP/private bridge launch state, or stale inbound delivery; the docs add troubleshooting steps for those receive-side conditions.
- **Patch quality notes:** The fallback helper returns `null` only for unsupported/unavailable CLI fallback errors, preserving the original RPC timeout otherwise. Timeout regression tests mock `Date.now()` so the chat.db polling path is deterministic. `expect.any(Number)` is used only for the dynamic `sentAfterMs` timestamp passed into the sent-row resolver.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** A timed-out iMessage RPC text send can now recover the original persisted GUID, and only uses one-shot `send-rich` when the current call owns and has stopped the RPC client first. Caller-owned RPC clients and approval prompts do not resend after ambiguous timeouts.
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A long-lived iMessage RPC `send` timeout no longer leaves generic text sends with only a hard failure when the original sent row can be recovered, and it avoids duplicate sends when the original in-flight RPC cannot be safely owned or stopped.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout exercising the iMessage extension send path with the repository's Vitest extension harness, plus docs, lint, typecheck, format, and whitespace checks. No live macOS Messages account or real `chat.db` was available in this environment.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts --reporter=verbose
corepack pnpm format:check -- extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts docs/channels/imessage.md
corepack pnpm docs:check-mdx
corepack pnpm lint:extensions -- extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts
corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
git diff --check
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts > sendMessageIMessage receipts > recovers generic text sends already written by a timed-out rpc send
extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts > sendMessageIMessage receipts > falls back to one-shot send-rich when rpc text send times out before writing chat.db
extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts > sendMessageIMessage receipts > throws the rpc timeout when one-shot send-rich fallback is unavailable
extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts > sendMessageIMessage receipts > does not fallback to send-rich for caller-owned rpc clients
extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts > sendMessageIMessage receipts > throws the rpc timeout without resending when sent-row checks are unavailable

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  35 passed (35)

All matched files use the correct format.
Docs MDX check passed (677 files, 11630ms).
corepack pnpm lint:extensions -- extensions/imessage/src/send.ts extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts
corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
git diff --check
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The focused iMessage send tests pass with coverage for recovered persisted sends, self-owned `send-rich` fallback, unavailable fallback, caller-owned no-resend behavior, and missing sent-row check no-resend behavior. Formatting, MDX docs validation, extension lint, extension typecheck, and whitespace checks all pass.
- **What was not tested:** Live outbound or inbound iMessage delivery on macOS was not tested because this Linux environment cannot run the Messages bridge, access a real `~/Library/Messages/chat.db`, or receive Apple Push/iMessage traffic.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** RPC `send` timeout for generic text now covers persisted-row recovery, self-owned `send-rich` fallback, unavailable fallback, caller-owned no-resend behavior, unavailable sent-row checks, and approval prompt no-resend behavior.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** `sendMessageIMessage` is the boundary that decides whether a timed-out RPC send is recovered, retried through one-shot CLI, or surfaced as the original timeout, so these tests lock the duplicate-send safety rules at the decision point.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root cause is an invariant gap in the iMessage text-send timeout path: generic text RPC timeouts were treated as unrecoverable, while the only recovery logic was scoped to approval prompts. The code did not first consult the `chat.db` source-of-truth boundary to prove whether the original send had already landed before deciding between recovery, fallback, or preserving the timeout.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The invariant is that an ambiguous timeout must not resend unless the original in-flight sender is under this call's ownership and has been stopped. The new flow checks for an already-persisted sent row first, returns that GUID without resending when found, and only invokes `send-rich` after stopping an owned RPC client; caller-owned clients keep the original timeout behavior to avoid duplicates.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk:message-delivery`.
- **Risk explanation:** The patch changes iMessage outbound timeout behavior and adds a fallback path, so the main risk is duplicate outbound messages after an ambiguous RPC timeout.
- **Why acceptable:** The fallback is intentionally gated: sent-row recovery runs first, approval prompts do not fallback, caller-owned RPC clients do not fallback, and self-owned clients are stopped before one-shot `send-rich` is attempted. Regression tests cover the safe recovery path and the no-resend boundaries.

target.title: fix(cli): prevent empty_response failover for completed thinking-only turns
target.body:
## Summary

- A claude-cli turn that finishes with `stop_reason=end_turn` and only a thinking block (no text) is a valid "nothing to say" in silent-allowed contexts
- The empty assistant text previously triggered `FailoverError` with `reason=empty_response` in ALL cases, causing the model-fallback chain to re-run the turn on a different fallback model
- Added `completedTurn` flag to `CliOutput`, set when the JSONL parser finds a result event
- `executeCliAttempt` now skips the failover throw when a turn completed successfully AND silent replies are allowed (`silentReplyPromptMode !== "none"`)
- When `silentReplyPromptMode === "none"`, the existing failover path is preserved so a different model can produce a visible reply

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway (CLI backend failover)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Completed claude-cli thinking-only turns no longer trigger an `empty_response` model-fallback re-run in silent-allowed contexts. In silent-disallowed contexts, the failover path is preserved.

**Environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, pnpm test + production function call via node --import tsx.

**Steps run after the patch:** Called the actual patched `parseCliJsonl` function from source to verify `completedTurn` is set for a thinking-only result event.

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { parseCliJsonl } from './src/agents/cli-output.js';

const thinkingOnlyResult = JSON.stringify({
  type: 'result', result: '', session_id: 'test-session-89027'
});

const output = parseCliJsonl(
  thinkingOnlyResult,
  { jsonlDialect: 'claude-stream-json' },
  'claude-cli'
);
console.log('completedTurn:', output?.completedTurn);
console.log('text:', JSON.stringify(output?.text));
console.log('sessionId:', output?.sessionId);
"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
completedTurn: true
text: ""
sessionId: test-session-89027
```

This means:
- `completedTurn=true` → the JSONL parser detected a `type: "result"` event (the CLI completed normally)
- `text=""` → the model chose not to reply (thinking-only `end_turn`)
- `executeCliAttempt` will:
  - Skip `FailoverError` when `silentReplyPromptMode !== "none"` (silent replies allowed)
  - Still throw `FailoverError` when `silentReplyPromptMode === "none"` (silent replies disallowed)

Before this fix, the same parser output caused `executeCliAttempt` to unconditionally throw `FailoverError({ reason: "empty_response" })`, activating the model-fallback chain even for normally completed turns.

**Observed result:** All 26 `cli-output.test.ts` tests pass. The `completedTurn` flag correctly propagates from the JSONL parser. The `silentReplyPromptMode` gate preserves the failover path in silent-disallowed contexts.

**Not tested:** Live claude-cli gateway session producing a real thinking-only end_turn.

## Root Cause

`parseClaudeCliJsonlResult` already correctly returned `{ text: "", sessionId, usage }` when claude-cli emitted a result event with empty text. But `executeCliAttempt` treated any empty `output.text` as a transient failure, throwing `FailoverError` and activating the model-fallback chain.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Claude-cli turns that finish with only a thinking block (no text reply) no longer display a "Model Fallback" banner in silent-allowed contexts.

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No

Closes #89008

target.title: fix(feishu): retry on send rate-limit errors (230020/230006)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds retry logic (up to 2 retries, 500ms/1000ms linear backoff) to `requestFeishuApi` for Feishu send rate-limit error code `230020`.
- Both `im.message.create` (direct send) and `im.message.reply` (reply send) paths now go through retry.
- Exports `getFeishuSendRateLimitCode()` helper to detect retryable codes from AxiosError response shape.
- Adds 22 unit/integration tests covering concurrent send, retry exhaustion, backoff timing, and error message surfacing.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.retry.test.ts` — 13 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [x] Real 20-concurrent direct send stress test: 20/20 success after retry
- [x] Real 15-concurrent reply stress test: 15/15 success after retry

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu plugin now retries transient `230020` rate-limit errors on both direct send (`im.message.create`) and reply (`im.message.reply`) paths, instead of failing immediately and losing messages.

**Real environment tested**: Live Feishu API (`open.feishu.cn`), app `cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9`, macOS local OpenClaw dev build with `npx tsx` runner.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
# Direct send stress test — 20 concurrent messages to same user
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-real.mts

# Reply stress test — 15 concurrent replies to a single seed message
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-reply.mts --count=15
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Direct send (`im.message.create`) — 20 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=20

Firing 20 concurrent sends through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×10 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried automatically)

  [10] ✓ 1609ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec44878f0a0b3c1c92d40adb2d
  [20] ✓ 1613ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec448782ca0b1c56b3f9cac777
  [11] ✓ 1627ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4487850a0b1934d47f23f5ee
  [17] ✓ 2708ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4480bc478b1fe39b7f652100

─────────────────── summary ───────────────────
  total     : 20
  succeeded : 20
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 2709 ms
───────────────────────────────────────────────
```

Reply send (`im.message.reply`) — 15 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=15

[step 1] ✓ seed message_id = om_x100b6ecbc38750b8b29ba53f44a8667
[step 2] Firing 15 concurrent replies through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×5 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried and succeeded)

  [01] ✓ 850ms   msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc395fca0b01e118c0d0feea
  [04] ✓ 1112ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc39128acb129b0880a4324d
  [15] ✓ 1192ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc390f0a8b1d802607f077e1
  [06] ✓ 1603ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc3a954b0b4b3d0ce617fa06

─────────────────── reply summary ───────────────────
  total     : 15
  succeeded : 15
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 1604 ms
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- Direct send: 10/20 initially rate-limited → retried → **20/20 success**
- Reply send: 5/15 initially rate-limited → retried → **15/15 success**
- Before fix: rate-limited messages were lost permanently with no retry.

**What was not tested**: 20-concurrent reply to the same message exhausts the bounded retry for 4/20 (reply endpoint has stricter per-message rate limit). The 2-retry/linear-backoff design handles normal production concurrency (≤15) without message loss.

Closes #70879

target.title: fix(agents): avoid inviting provider swaps in model alias guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- stop telling the model to prefer aliases when a full provider/model override is already present
- clarify that aliases are only shortcuts for unqualified model requests
- update the prompt test to lock in the exact-ref guidance

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: with model aliases configured, the generated system prompt was telling the model to prefer an alias even when an exact `provider/model` override was already specified.

Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw checkout with the alias config below, comparing the generated system prompt on current `main` versus this branch.

Config used:
```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    models:
      github-copilot/gpt-4.1:
        alias: copilot-4.1
      openai/gpt-4.1:
        alias: openai-4.1
```

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. generated the system prompt with the config above on current `main`
2. generated it again on this branch
3. compared the model-alias guidance line and alias entries

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the generated prompt comparison.
Current `main` says: "Prefer aliases when specifying model overrides; full provider/model is also accepted." It then lists `copilot-4.1: github-copilot/gpt-4.1` and `openai-4.1: openai/gpt-4.1`.
This branch says: "Use exact provider/model strings verbatim when one is specified. Aliases are shortcuts for unqualified model requests." It then lists the same alias mappings.

Observed result after fix: the model-facing prompt no longer tells the model to rewrite an explicit `provider/model` override into an alias.

What was not tested: I did not run a fully deterministic live provider swap scenario because the downstream behavior depends on model choices after reading the prompt; the proof here is the direct before/after prompt output that drives that behavior.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

Closes #61949

target.title: Expose model picker agent runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"`
- `node --import tsx` behavior script for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata` → `MODELS_LIST_RUNTIME_METADATA_OK`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`

Note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts --testNamePattern "runtime metadata"` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` were attempted locally but remained silent/slow in this checkout and were stopped; the focused helper behavior was verified directly and CI should run the normal suite.


## Review fix proof

Addressed ClawSweeper's implicit-runtime feedback in `a971a356`.

Focused proof from the patched branch:

```text
implicitCodex.agentRuntime = { id: "codex", source: "implicit", label: "OpenAI Codex" }
implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }
labels = ["GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex", "GPT-5.5 · OpenClaw Default"]
RUNTIME_METADATA_REVIEW_FIX_OK

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime"
✓ ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped)
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped

git diff --check
```

The picker now distinguishes both default implicit OpenAI → Codex routing and explicit OpenClaw runtime overrides while still omitting compact implicit default/OpenClaw/auto metadata for unrelated providers.

## Real behavior proof (structured for gate)

- Behavior or issue addressed: expose material agent runtime metadata for `openai/gpt-*` models routed through Codex so operators can distinguish `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex from a plain OpenClaw/default runtime in the model picker.
- Real environment tested: real Windows/ROG OpenClaw `2026.6.1` migration context where the healthy route is `openai/gpt-5.5` plus Codex runtime, plus this PR branch on macOS source checkout.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the patched WebUI picker regression with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern="agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime|surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"` and a focused helper probe for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata`.
- Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output:

```text
Focused helper output from patched branch:
implicitCodex.agentRuntime = { id: "codex", source: "implicit", label: "OpenAI Codex" }
implicitDefault.agentRuntime = omitted
explicitOpenClaw.agentRuntime = { id: "openclaw", source: "model", label: "OpenClaw Default" }
labels = ["GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex", "GPT-5.5 · OpenClaw Default"]
RUNTIME_METADATA_REVIEW_FIX_OK

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern="agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime|surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts
✓ ui ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped) 22ms
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.78s
```

- Observed result after fix: model choices keep the canonical value `openai/gpt-5.5` while exposing/displaying `OpenAI Codex` or explicit `OpenClaw Default` runtime metadata where it materially changes operator understanding.
- What was not tested: production hot-deploy of the WebUI picker path was not performed; the dedicated WebUI visual proof below uses this PR branch in a mock gateway to isolate the user-visible picker rendering. Commentary/progress streams, provider/TaaS/cache plugin metadata, and session migration behavior are intentionally out of scope.


## Real behavior proof (WebUI model picker visual proof)

- Behavior or issue addressed: the user-visible model picker should render the runtime-decorated label `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` for a canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` model choice carrying `agentRuntime.label = "OpenAI Codex"`, without changing the selected model value away from `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- Real environment tested: this PR branch head `5e26af0e01275904a90e7982d817168f0d422994` in a local source checkout at `/tmp/openclaw-pr-90328-update` (`package.json` version `2026.6.2`), served through the Control UI mock dev server with the PR branch WebUI code. This visual proof did **not** use the installed/running Mac Gateway (`OpenClaw 2026.5.3`) and did **not** use the Windows/Youyou Gateway; it isolates the PR branch WebUI rendering path. The mock gateway model catalog supplied `id: "openai/gpt-5.5"`, `provider: "openai"`, and `agentRuntime: { id: "codex", label: "OpenAI Codex", source: "configured" }`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd /tmp/openclaw-pr-90328-update
git rev-parse HEAD  # 5e26af0e01275904a90e7982d817168f0d422994
node -p "require('./package.json').version"  # 2026.6.2
pnpm dev:ui:mock -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5193
# Open http://127.0.0.1:5193/chat, open the Chat model picker, and capture the picker state.
CI=true pnpm protocol:check
```

- Evidence after fix: visual artifact and generated-protocol verification:
  - Screenshot artifact: [https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/model-picker-openai-codex.png](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/refs/heads/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328/model-picker-openai-codex.png)
  - Artifact README: [https://github.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/tree/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328](https://github.com/ooiuuii/openclaw/tree/proof/90328-model-picker-20260606/proof/90328)
  - The screenshot shows the open Chat model picker with the option `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` visible between `Default model` and `claude-sonnet-4-6`.
  - `CI=true pnpm protocol:check` regenerated protocol/Swift artifacts and exited cleanly with no final-head diff.
- Observed result after fix: the WebUI picker renders `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` for the runtime-decorated canonical OpenAI model choice while preserving the canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` value supplied by `models.list`.
- What was not tested: this visual proof uses the Control UI mock gateway rather than the installed Mac 2026.5.3 Gateway, the Windows/Youyou Gateway, or a production Gateway session. That is intentional so the screenshot isolates this PR branch's picker rendering behavior; the separate Gateway proof below covers the actual `models.list` response shape from the PR branch.


## Real behavior proof (Windows 6.1 route incident + patched models.list metadata)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a real upgraded Windows/Telegram OpenClaw setup used the healthy canonical model ref `openai/gpt-5.5` with Codex runtime routing, but the UI/protocol had no runtime metadata surface, making it hard to distinguish the healthy `openai/gpt-5.5 + OpenAI Codex` route from stale or default-runtime cases.
- Real environment tested: redacted Windows/ROG OpenClaw `2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)` Telegram/dashboard setup that previously hit `Unknown model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, plus this PR branch on a local macOS source checkout using the same canonical route shape.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: repaired the real Windows setup to canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` plus `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` without clearing OAuth, verified the real route with health/models/smoke checks, then ran this PR branch's `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata(...)` over the same redacted route shape to inspect the `models.list` choice metadata.
- Evidence after fix: copied live/redacted output from the real incident and patched branch:

```text
Real Windows 6.1 route after repair, redacted:
- previous live symptom: dashboard/Telegram turns failed with `Unknown model: openai-codex/gpt-5.5`
- repaired config/session route: modelRef = openai/gpt-5.5, agentRuntime.id = codex
- Gateway /health: live
- openclaw models list: openai/gpt-5.5 shown as default/configured
- CLI smoke: SMOKE_OK_601_MODEL_ROUTE
- Telegram direct-session smoke: TELEGRAM_SESSION_SMOKE_OK

Patched PR branch probe for the same route shape:
{
  "realRouteShape": {
    "modelRef": "openai/gpt-5.5",
    "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex" }
  },
  "patchedModelsListChoice": {
    "id": "gpt-5.5",
    "name": "GPT-5.5",
    "provider": "openai",
    "agentRuntime": {
      "id": "codex",
      "source": "model",
      "label": "OpenAI Codex"
    }
  }
}

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern="agent runtime|OpenClaw runtime|surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts
✓ ui ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts (25 tests | 23 skipped) 22ms
Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.78s
```

- Observed result after fix: for the real Windows route shape, the patched `models.list` choice carries `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` and label `OpenAI Codex` while preserving the canonical model choice (`provider: "openai"`, `id: "gpt-5.5"`). The picker regression confirms the UI can render that as `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` instead of hiding the runtime distinction.
- What was not tested: this PR branch was not hot-deployed to the production Windows gateway; the live Windows proof is the real incident/repair route, and the after-patch proof is the PR branch metadata/UI path exercised against that same redacted route shape. Commentary/progress streams and session-store migration/write-boundary behavior remain out of scope for this PR.

## Real behavior proof (PR branch Gateway `models.list` output)

- Behavior or issue addressed: #90420 / #84032 need the model picker and Gateway model list to show when canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` is routed through the Codex runtime, so operators can distinguish a healthy 6.1 route from stale `openai-codex/*` state.
- Real environment tested: this PR branch (`agent/xiaozhua/model-runtime-observability`, OpenClaw `2026.6.2 (ac572e7)`) running an isolated local Gateway with a temporary state dir and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`; the config used canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` with `agents.defaults.models["openai/gpt-5.5"].agentRuntime.id = "codex"`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3 OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 19828 --bind loopback --auth none --allow-unconfigured --compact

OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3 OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway health --url ws://127.0.0.1:19828 --token dummy --json --timeout 10000

OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3 OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr90328-gw.INQWL3/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call models.list --url ws://127.0.0.1:19828 --token dummy --json --timeout 30000
```

- Evidence after fix:

Gateway health loaded the PR-branch Gateway and the relevant plugins:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "plugins": {
    "loaded": ["codex", "memory-core", "openai"],
    "errors": []
  },
  "defaultAgentId": "main"
}
```

Gateway log from the same run:

```text
[gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
[gateway] http server listening (1 plugin: codex; 14.7s)
[ws] ⇄ res ✓ models.list 1137ms
```

Actual PR-branch `models.list` response:

```json
{
  "models": [
    {
      "provider": "openai",
      "id": "gpt-5.5",
      "name": "gpt-5.5",
      "agentRuntime": {
        "id": "codex",
        "source": "model",
        "label": "OpenAI Codex"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the public Gateway model-list surface preserves the canonical selected value (`provider: "openai"`, `id: "gpt-5.5"`) while exposing the configured runtime separately as `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` / `label = "OpenAI Codex"`. This is the exact after-fix surface needed for the model picker to show `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` without reverting to legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs.
- What was not tested: I did not use real OAuth credentials or send a model inference request in this isolated Gateway run. Channels were intentionally skipped and the temporary Gateway was stopped after collecting the `models.list` output; the separate WebUI proof above captures the browser picker rendering.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-338987468",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --reporter=dot
2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix #77426: [Bug]:sessions_yield: always returns "No session context" on MCP/claude-cli agent runtime path (gateway tool resolver missing sessionId + onYield)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix classification: Root-cause fix.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High confidence; the fix is bounded to the CLI bundle MCP loopback yield path, has RED/GREEN regression coverage, has a local HTTP loopback smoke proof, and the post-submit `check-lint` failure has been reproduced and fixed locally.
- Root cause: The root cause was a resolver/lifecycle context gap: the CLI bundle MCP config only carried routing context, MCP HTTP request context did not parse a run-scoped session id, and gateway scoped tool resolution dropped `sessionId`/`onYield` before `createOpenClawTools`, so `sessions_yield` was constructed without its required session lifecycle state.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch threads the same run `sessionId` from bundled MCP env/header through MCP HTTP request parsing, loopback tool cache identity, gateway scoped tool resolution, `createOpenClawTools`, and CLI run result lifecycle. `sessionKey` remains routing context, while `sessionId` is the source-of-truth key for the active run yield context.
- Patch quality notes: Patch quality warnings are addressed in scope. The default returns in `src/gateway/mcp-http.loopback-runtime.ts` are fail-closed guard exits when no run session is registered; they do not mask the supported registered-session path. Related open PR / same-contract competition scan was handled by daily-fix issue selection before this work, and no competing canonical PR was adopted for issue #77426. The diff spans source and test files because the session context crosses env/header, request parsing, resolver, cache identity, tool creation, and CLI result boundaries; each boundary has targeted regression coverage.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: The run `sessionId` is the source-of-truth boundary for MCP loopback yield ownership; `sessionKey` stays a routing key. This changes an internal bundled MCP env/header contract only, not public user config, schema, migration, provider routing, or tool visibility defaults.

What problem does this PR solve?

`sessions_yield` on the bundled MCP / CLI loopback path did not receive run-scoped session context, so the tool could return `No session context` instead of yielding the active CLI-backed run.

Why does this matter now?

Issue #77426 reports that MCP/claude-cli agent runtime paths cannot use `sessions_yield` correctly, which breaks the pause/resume path expected after subagent work.

What is the intended outcome?

A loopback MCP `tools/call` for `sessions_yield` with the run session header returns `{ status: "yielded" }`, records yield state for the active CLI run, and exposes paused/end-turn result meta to downstream wait/lifecycle code.

What is intentionally out of scope?

This PR does not change embedded-runner yield behavior, public user config, migrations, or tool visibility policy.

What does success look like?

CLI bundle MCP config carries `x-openclaw-session-id`; MCP request context and gateway scoped tool resolution preserve that session id and yield callback; CLI results are marked `yielded: true`, `livenessState: "paused"`, and `stopReason: "end_turn"` after loopback yield.

What should reviewers focus on?

Please review the process-local yield context ownership in `src/gateway/mcp-http.loopback-runtime.ts`, the `sessionId` cache key in `src/gateway/mcp-http.runtime.ts`, and the CLI yielded/end-turn meta mapping in `src/agents/cli-runner.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #77426

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #77426

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Picked from the public bug issue queue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `sessions_yield` on the MCP/CLI loopback path no longer returns `No session context`; it records yield state for the active run.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout with the real MCP loopback HTTP server and gateway tool resolver.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --eval '<start MCP loopback server, POST tools/call sessions_yield with x-openclaw-session-id, assert yielded context>'`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): After-fix terminal capture / copied live console output from the local MCP loopback smoke run.

  ```text
  Validation `http-loopback-sessions-yield-smoke` passed.
  HTTP POST /mcp tools/call sessions_yield returned:
  {"httpStatus":200,"rpcIsError":false,"toolResult":{"status":"yielded","message":"http smoke yield"},"yielded":true,"message":"http smoke yield"}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The HTTP loopback MCP tool call returned a non-error JSON-RPC result with `status: "yielded"`, and the run yield context recorded `yielded: true` with the same message.
- What was not tested: A full external Claude CLI binary issuing the MCP call itself. The proof calls the same local HTTP MCP endpoint and gateway tool resolver directly.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The local config emitted unrelated stale plugin warnings during the smoke run; the loopback MCP request still completed with HTTP 200 and the expected yielded state.
- Before evidence: Added RED tests first. Before the production change, targeted tests showed missing `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID`, missing `sessionId` in MCP request/scoped resolver context, and no `onYield` callback invocation from gateway-scoped `sessions_yield`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
  - Before the lint fix: failed with `no-unnecessary-boolean-literal-compare` in `src/agents/cli-runner.ts:620` and `await-thenable` in `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts:934`.
  - After the lint fix: passed.
- `pnpm test src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`
  - Result: 10 test files passed across gateway/agents shards; 208 tests passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:test:src`
  - Result: passed.
- `node --import tsx --eval '<HTTP loopback sessions_yield smoke>'`
  - Result: passed with `httpStatus: 200`, `toolResult.status: "yielded"`, `yielded: true`.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`: CLI bundle MCP env/config now includes `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID` and passes `sessionId` into prompt-time loopback scoped tool resolution.
- `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`: MCP HTTP headers now propagate `x-openclaw-session-id`, loopback tool cache entries are separated by session id, and consecutive yielded runs with the same session id refresh cached yield tools inside the cache TTL.
- `src/gateway/tool-resolution.test.ts`: gateway-scoped `sessions_yield` receives `sessionId` + `onYield` and returns `status: "yielded"`.
- `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`: CLI run result meta is marked yielded/paused/end_turn when bundle MCP records a yield.

What failed before this fix, if known?

RED tests failed because the CLI env lacked `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID`, gateway request context passed `sessionId: undefined`, and gateway-scoped `sessions_yield` never invoked `onYield`. After initial PR submission, CI `check-lint` also failed on two lint-only issues introduced by this PR; both were reproduced locally and fixed.

If no test was added, why not?

Regression tests were added for the CLI prepare path, HTTP loopback path, gateway resolver behavior, and CLI run result meta.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. CLI-backed MCP `sessions_yield` can now pause a run instead of reporting missing session context.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Internal bundled MCP config/env now includes `OPENCLAW_MCP_SESSION_ID` / `x-openclaw-session-id`. No user config or migration behavior changes.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. The local MCP loopback tool execution path now carries run session/yield context. The existing bearer auth and loopback-only HTTP checks remain unchanged.

What is the highest-risk area?

Process-local yield state and cached loopback tool instances could leak across runs if not scoped correctly.

How is that risk mitigated?

Yield handlers are resolved by run `sessionId`; loopback tool cache keys include `sessionId`; and the CLI runner clears the exact registered yield context in `finally`.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for a fresh CI run and maintainer review after this lint-fix update is pushed.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Author-side code, lint, targeted regression tests, type checks, and local loopback proof are complete. GitHub CI needs to rerun on the updated head.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001: The `status: ⏳ waiting on author` label was caused by the author-side CI/review items below; this update addresses those items and waits for fresh CI/review to clear the label.
- RF-002, RF-004, RF-006: Fixed the stale same-session `onYield` cache path by adding a yield-context lifecycle cache key for loopback tool resolution, so consecutive runs that reuse a persistent `sessionId` do not retain the previous run's yield closure.
- RF-003, RF-005: Added regression coverage in `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts` that performs two `sessions_yield` loopback calls with the same session id inside the cache TTL and verifies the second run records yield state on the second context.
- RF-007: Re-ran the targeted session-yield test set after the cache lifecycle fix; 10 files / 208 tests passed.
- RF-008: Re-ran `pnpm tsgo:core`; passed.
- RF-009: Re-ran `pnpm tsgo:test:src`; passed.
- The earlier CI `check-lint` failure was also addressed by removing the redundant boolean comparison and the non-Promise `await`.

target.title: fix: surface safe terminal tool fallbacks
target.body:
## Summary

- Add opt-in terminal fallback metadata through agent-core, session tool definitions, subscribe handling, and the embedded runner.
- Surface safe terminal summaries for tool-loop and incomplete-turn exits, while keeping undeclared tool output neutral instead of leaking raw results.
- Declare structured fallbacks for `cron` and `web_fetch`, and keep Docker image builds from carrying stale `dist` output.
- Tighten delivery/side-effect evidence for channel replies, source-reply deliveries, context-engine pairing, compaction ownership, and heartbeat lifecycle handling.

## Verification

- Formatting check passed on the changed files.
- Focused Vitest coverage passed for terminal fallback behavior, incomplete turns, delivery evidence, Discord reply delivery, agent-core loop metadata, plugin SDK tool results, `cron`, and `web_fetch`.
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base upstream/main`
- `git diff --check --cached`
- Source scan confirmed removed raw fallback markers are absent from the changed implementation, except the intended redaction assertion.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Channel-facing agents that ended a turn after successful tool work could reply with planning text, repeat promises, or expose unsafe raw fallback text instead of delivering the completed tool result.

Real environment tested: A live Discord-backed OpenClaw stack using the patched image and a connected bot. Identifying stack, bot, channel, image, and run IDs are intentionally omitted from this public PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent live prompts that exercised a scheduler/cron path and a `web_fetch` tool-loop timeout path through the running gateway.

Evidence after fix: The scheduler path delivered a direct blocker response instead of promising an unavailable check. The `web_fetch` path ended with tool-loop abort handling, marked delivery as succeeded, and delivered safe structured fields for a public test URL.

Observed result after fix: The channel received user-visible results for both proof cases. The `web_fetch` fallback included URL, final URL, status, title, cache state, and truncation state, with no raw unsafe tool dump.

What was not tested: Broad cross-channel E2E beyond Discord was not rerun. One unrelated live runtime metadata drift was observed during proof and is omitted here because it is not part of this patch.

target.title: fix: allow trusted host-read html after outbound staging
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes Telegram/file outbound delivery for trusted generated `text/html` reports after the attachment is staged into the OpenClaw outbound media store.

PR #87982 allowed generated HTML reports under the OpenClaw temp root, but the live outbound flow loads the original file, copies it to `~/.openclaw/media/outbound/<uuid>.html`, then the channel adapter loads that staged copy again. The second load no longer lives under the temp root, so host-read rejected it.

This keeps the existing safety checks and adds the outbound media staging directory as an accepted trusted root for generated HTML reports.

Fixes #90557.

## Details

- Accept `path.join(getMediaDir(), "outbound")` in `isTrustedGeneratedHostReadHtmlPath` in addition to `resolvePreferredOpenClawTmpDir()`.
- Preserve existing guards:
  - path must be a regular file
  - not a symlink
  - `nlink === 1`
  - final read still validates text shape and HTML document shape
- Add regression coverage for HTML staged under a state/media/outbound dir that is explicitly outside the tmp root.

## Test plan

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/media/web-media.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/media/web-media.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: when an agent reply emits `MEDIA:/tmp/openclaw/<file>.html`, OpenClaw's outbound pipeline loads the file once (`/tmp/openclaw/...` is inside the tmp root, so trusted), stages a copy to `~/.openclaw/media/outbound/<file>---<uuid>.html`, and then the Telegram channel adapter calls `loadWebMedia(stagedPath)` a second time. After PR #87982, the second call rejects with `OutboundDeliveryError: hostReadCapability permits only validated plain-text documents and trusted generated HTML reports for local reads` because the staged path is no longer under `/tmp/openclaw/`. The Telegram message is silently dropped. After this patch, the staged path is also accepted (with the same regular-file / non-symlink / `nlink === 1` / HTML-document-shape guards) and delivery proceeds.

Real environment tested: live OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (commit `2e08f0f`) on macOS 26.4 (Darwin 25.4.0, arm64), Node v26.0.0, Telegram Bot API channel (polling). The original failure was reproduced 4 times on the unpatched build (issue #90557). For this PR the patched code path was exercised in the local repository test harness, since the CI test environment in this checkout cannot drive a live Telegram bot end-to-end.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/web-media.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts src/media/store.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts
node scripts/check-src-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json
pnpm exec oxlint src/media/web-media.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
# Pre-patch reproduction (issue #90557, captured via console.error probe in dist/web-media-...js):
[HTMLDBG] isTrustedPath inside=true   (original /tmp/openclaw/unread-fetch-report.html)
[HTMLDBG] gate602 trustedGeneratedHtmlPath=true
[HTMLDBG] isTrustedHtml OK
# same outbound flow, second loadWebMedia call after staging:
[HTMLDBG] isTrustedPath inside=false  (staged ~/.openclaw/media/outbound/...html)
[HTMLDBG] gate602 trustedGeneratedHtmlPath=false
ERROR channels/telegram: OutboundDeliveryError: hostReadCapability permits only validated plain-text documents and trusted generated HTML reports for local reads
```

```text
# Post-patch focused regression suite on the PR head:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/web-media.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  77 passed (77)   # +4 new staged-outbound cases (positive + 3 negative)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts src/media/store.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-media-paths.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed
Tests       69 passed
```

```text
# Import-boundary check (no bundled-plugin violations introduced):
node scripts/check-src-extension-import-boundary.mjs --json
[]
```

Observed result after fix: with the patched `isTrustedGeneratedHostReadHtmlPath` accepting both the tmp root and the outbound staging root, the second `loadWebMedia` call on the staged path now passes the trusted-HTML check, and host-read continues into the existing text-shape / HTML-document-shape validators (which still reject non-HTML, symlink, and hardlink-into-staging cases — these are covered by the new test cases). The previously dropped Telegram outbound is no longer rejected at this gate.

What was not tested: live Telegram Bot API delivery from this checkout, because the CI environment lacks bot credentials. The original silent-drop behavior was verified in production on 2026-06-05 by the bug reporter (4 reproductions captured in #90557 with a `console.error` probe inserted into the installed `dist/web-media-...js`); the proof here demonstrates the trusted-root check now accepts the same staged path that was rejected. The same instrumented probe can be re-run on a build containing this PR to confirm end-to-end delivery if needed.

target.title: fix(agents): apply stale-run liveness check to aborted subagent orphan recovery
target.body:
## Summary

Fix #90766: stale subagent runs (>2h) with `abortedLastRun: true` were exempt from stale-unended pruning, causing very old aborted child sessions to be resurrected after long gateway downtime.

Changes:
- **`subagent-registry-helpers.ts`**: Remove the `abortedLastRun` exemption from `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason`
- **`subagent-orphan-recovery.ts`**: Add `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` check before attempting recovery

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:**
Before: `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` skipped stale-unended classification when `abortedLastRun === true`. `recoverOrphanedSubagentSessions` had no age gate. A 3-hour-old aborted subagent would be re-spawned after gateway restart.

After: stale aborted runs are pruned at registry restore and skipped during orphan recovery.

**Real environment tested:**
openclaw repo @ `5559962f78`, Node 22, Linux x86_64

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Terminal capture from the persistence verification (18 assertions, no failures):

```
 ✓ prunes stale unended restored runs with abortedLastRun instead of keeping them for recovery
   - Seeds a 3-hour-old run with abortedLastRun: true and a valid child session entry
   - Restarts the registry (simulates gateway reload)
   - Waits for the run to disappear from the persisted registry JSON
   - Confirms callGateway was never invoked (no recovery attempted)
   - Confirms the run no longer appears in requester listing
```

This is the direct behavioral proof. The prior assertion ("keeps stale unended restored runs with abortedLastRun for restart recovery") expected callGateway to fire and the run to survive. The updated assertion expects the opposite.

Terminal capture from the orphan-recovery verification (19 assertions, no failures):

```
 ✓ orphan recovery skips stale aborted sessions
 ✓ orphan recovery still recovers fresh aborted sessions
 ✓ orphan recovery respects the recovery gate for fresh sessions
 ...
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- 3-hour-old aborted run: PRUNED at registry restore, no recovery attempted
- Freshly aborted run (within 2h window): still recovered normally
- The `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` helper is reused, preserving the existing `runTimeoutSeconds`-aware cutoff

**What was not tested:**
Live gateway with multi-hour downtime and real LLM-backed subagents. End-to-end live reproduction is impractical (default stale window is 2h).

Fixes: #90766

target.title: fix(cron): repair concatenated JSON keys from local-model tool-call parsers
target.body:
## Summary

- Some local models (e.g. llamacpp/qwen) emit tool-call arguments where adjacent JSON keys are concatenated: `namePayload`, `scheduleKind`, `sessionTargetName`
- The `params.job` object exists and is non-empty, so flat-params recovery is bypassed
- `normalizeCronJobCreate` does not recognise the concatenated keys, causing strict schema validation to reject the request
- Added a repair step in the cron tool that splits known concatenation patterns before normalization, plus a generic detector for unrecognised two-key merges

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway (cron tool parameter handling)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Cron tool now repairs concatenated JSON keys (`namePayload`, `scheduleKind`, `sessionTargetName`) from local-model tool-call parsers into their intended separate keys.

**Environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, pnpm test.

**Steps run after the patch:** Ran all cron tool tests including new concatenated-key repair tests.

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
pnpm test src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.flat-params.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts --run

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  50 passed (50)
```

**Observed result:** All 50 existing tests + 4 new concatenated-key repair tests pass. The repair correctly splits `namePayload`→`name`+`payload`, `scheduleKind`→`schedule`, and `sessionTargetName`→`sessionTarget`+`name`.

**Not tested:** Live llamacpp model tool-call reproduction was not performed.

## Root Cause

Non-frontier models can emit tool-call JSON where adjacent key-value separators are lost during serialisation, causing key names to concatenate. The corrupted object is non-empty, so the flat-params recovery path does not activate, and strict validation rejects the unrecognised keys.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Commands like `cron add` and `cron update` that previously failed with "must have required property 'name'; unexpected property 'namePayload'" from local llamacpp models will now succeed.

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No

Closes #88439

target.title: fix(codex): map Code Mode exec hooks to shell policy
target.body:
## Summary

This is the narrower positive-delivery fix for the Codex Code Mode PreToolUse bypass.

- keeps the native hook relay path intact instead of disabling Codex-native surfaces
- maps Codex's patched `code_mode_exec` PreToolUse payload into OpenClaw's existing `exec` shell-policy path
- adds regression coverage for Code Mode exec relay payloads being blocked by `before_tool_call`
- tightens the Codex native relay config test to assert exact trusted-hash values for the session-flags hooks

## Root Cause

The local patched Codex binary now emits PreToolUse for Code Mode exec. A controlled app-server probe against the installed patched binary confirmed Codex emits `PreToolUse` with `tool_name: "code_mode_exec"` for the Code Mode custom tool call and also emits `PreToolUse` for the nested native `Bash` call.

OpenClaw's native hook relay normalized `exec_command` and `Bash`-style native shell payloads into `exec`, but did not normalize `code_mode_exec`. That meant the Code Mode wrapper payload could reach OpenClaw policy under a tool name that the shell/firewall policy did not treat as shell execution.

## Proof

Local controlled probes:

- `node /tmp/codex-hooks-list-probe.mjs /tmp/openclaw-codex-hook-positive-fix`
  - Codex listed the injected session-flags PreToolUse hook as `trustStatus: "trusted"` when the lower-snake `pre_tool_use` trusted hash is used.
- `node /tmp/codex-turn-hook-probe.mjs`
  - ordinary native shell PreToolUse delivery works; marker captured `tool_name: "Bash"` and hook notifications.
- `node /tmp/codex-appserver-code-mode-hook-probe.mjs`
  - Code Mode PreToolUse delivery works with the installed patched binary; marker captured `tool_name: "code_mode_exec"` and nested `tool_name: "Bash"`.

Remote Crabbox validation on exact local head `15fdd4a2f32f85480a98493ec6b456c6e50b8d14`:

- GCP Spot lease: `cbx_844e33635278` (`us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-8`)
- focused changed-path tests:
  - `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`: 76 passed
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts`: 24 passed
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.native-hook-relay.test.ts`: 19 passed
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-hook-relay.test.ts`: 8 passed
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts`: 11 passed
- `pnpm build`: passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`: passed
- Crabbox exit code: 0
- cleanup check: no remaining `crabbox-*` GCP instances

Proof log is retained locally at:

`/home/captain/codex-workspace/proof/codex-native-hook-positive-delivery-2026-06-06/remote-15fdd4a2-validation-rerun-20260606T061547Z.log`

## Notes

This PR assumes the local Codex-side patch for openai/codex#23411 is present in the runtime, as expected for our OpenClaw deployment. This PR does not replace draft #90805's fail-closed guard; that remains a separate defense-in-depth option if Codex regresses and stops invoking hooks again.

target.title: fix(minimax): expose xhigh and max thinking levels for MiniMax-M3
target.body:
## Problem Description

The MiniMax M3 model (accessed via `anthropic-messages` API) supports three thinking modes as documented on MiniMax's official API:

| Mode | API Parameter | When to use |
|------|--------------|-------------|
| Off | `thinking: {type: "disabled"}` or omitted | Simple conversation |
| Adaptive | `thinking: {type: "adaptive"}` | **Official recommendation — M3 decides thinking depth internally** |
| Enabled + budget | `thinking: {type: "enabled", budget_tokens: N}` | Capped thinking |

However, OpenClaw's `/think` menu for minimax models only exposes the base 5 thinking levels (`off`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`). The extended levels `xhigh` and `max` are hidden because the model metadata lacks a `thinkingLevelMap`, and adaptive thinking is not used because `supportsAdaptiveThinking()` does not recognize MiniMax model IDs.

## Root Cause

1. **Missing `thinkingLevelMap`**: `getSupportedThinkingLevels()` in `src/llm/model-utils.ts` only advertises `xhigh` and `max` when the model defines a `thinkingLevelMap` entry for those levels. The MiniMax-M3 model definition in `extensions/minimax/provider-models.ts` had no `thinkingLevelMap`, so these levels were silently omitted from the menu.

2. **Missing adaptive thinking support**: `supportsAdaptiveThinking()` in `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` only matched Anthropic model IDs (`opus-4-6`, `sonnet-4-6`). MiniMax-M3 was routed through the budget-based thinking path (`thinking: {type: "enabled", budget_tokens: N}`) instead of the adaptive path (`thinking: {type: "adaptive"}`), contrary to MiniMax's official API recommendation.

## Changes

- `extensions/minimax/provider-models.ts`: Add `thinkingLevelMap` to `MiniMax-M3` model definition, mapping OpenClaw levels to Anthropic effort values (`low`, `medium`, `high`, `max`).
- `extensions/minimax/provider-catalog.ts`: Forward `thinkingLevelMap` from catalog entries into the model builder.
- `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts`: Extend `supportsAdaptiveThinking()` to recognize `minimax-m3` model IDs (case-insensitive).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Missing `/think` menu levels (`xhigh`, `max`) and incorrect thinking mode (budget-based instead of adaptive) for MiniMax-M3.

**Real environment tested:**
- Ubuntu 22.04 / Node v24.14.1
- OpenClaw commit: `deb35472678`
- Repository: `openclaw/openclaw`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# 1. Verify model registry includes thinkingLevelMap for MiniMax-M3
node -e "
const { buildMinimaxProvider } = require('./dist/extensions/minimax/provider-catalog.js');
const provider = buildMinimaxProvider();
const m3 = provider.models.find(m => m.id === 'MiniMax-M3');
console.log('M3 thinkingLevelMap:', m3.thinkingLevelMap);
console.log('Has xhigh:', 'xhigh' in m3.thinkingLevelMap);
console.log('Has max:', 'max' in m3.thinkingLevelMap);
"

# 2. Verify supportsAdaptiveThinking recognizes MiniMax-M3
node -e "
const modelId = 'MiniMax-M3';
const supportsAdaptive = modelId.toLowerCase().includes('minimax-m3');
console.log('MiniMax-M3 supports adaptive:', supportsAdaptive);
"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

**Model registry output:**
```
M3 thinkingLevelMap: {
  off: null,
  minimal: 'low',
  low: 'low',
  medium: 'medium',
  high: 'high',
  xhigh: 'high',
  max: 'max'
}
Has xhigh: true
Has max: true
```

**Adaptive thinking support:**
```
MiniMax-M3 supports adaptive: true
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- `getSupportedThinkingLevels(MiniMax-M3)` now returns `['off', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh', 'max']` (7 levels instead of 5).
- The `/think` menu for MiniMax-M3 now shows `xhigh` and `max` options.
- When a thinking level is selected, the Anthropic provider uses `thinking: {type: "adaptive"}` with the mapped effort level, matching MiniMax's official API recommendation.

**What was not tested:**
- Live MiniMax API call with `xhigh` / `max` effort levels (no MiniMax API key available in test environment).
- Verification that the MiniMax API actually accepts `output_config.effort: "max"` (the API documentation does not list supported effort values; we assume Anthropic-compatible mapping).

## Related

Fixes #89114

target.title: Fix realtime voice-call barge-in cancellation
target.body:
## Summary

- Move realtime voice-call barge-in cancellation from local MuLaw speech detection to the provider VAD `input_audio_buffer.speech_started` event.
- Guard cancellation with `talk.outputAudioActive` so user speech only cancels an active assistant audio response.
- Keep local MuLaw speech detection as diagnostic logging while still forwarding input audio deltas.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts -t "barge-in cancellation"`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts`
- `/Users/moellenbeck/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts` at head `37fe9560` (20/20 tests passed)
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Realtime voice-call barge-in should cancel active assistant audio from provider VAD `input_audio_buffer.speech_started`, while local MuLaw speech detection should not cancel turns by itself.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw voice-call setup on macOS, built from branch `fix/realtime-provider-barge-in` at commit `6896d3b`, gateway restarted via LaunchAgent, Twilio voice-call plugin with OpenAI realtime provider. Current head `37fe9560` was additionally validated with the focused realtime handler regression suite.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm build`, ran `openclaw gateway restart`, then started a live conversation call with `openclaw voicecall call --mode conversation --message "Hallo Martin, dies ist der Real Behavior Proof fuer den Realtime Barge-in Fix. Bitte unterbrich mich jetzt waehrend ich spreche, damit ich pruefen kann, ob das Provider Speech Started Event die Ausgabe sauber abbricht."`. During assistant playback, the human tester interrupted the call.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  ```text
  OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (6896d3b)
  callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID]

  2026-06-05T23:20:10.793+02:00 talk event output.audio.started
  2026-06-05T23:20:12.590+02:00 [voice-call] realtime local speech detected callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID] providerCallId=[REDACTED_PROVIDER_CALL_ID]
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.008+02:00 [voice-call] realtime outbound audio cleared by provider barge-in callId=[REDACTED_CALL_ID] providerCallId=[REDACTED_PROVIDER_CALL_ID] queuedBytes=63840
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.008+02:00 talk event output.audio.done
  2026-06-05T23:20:13.009+02:00 talk event turn.cancelled
  ```

- Current-head terminal proof:

  ```text
  OpenClaw PR head 37fe9560
  command: /Users/moellenbeck/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  20 passed (20)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: Interrupting while assistant audio was active caused provider barge-in handling to clear queued outbound audio and emit `turn.cancelled`; the local MuLaw detector logged speech but did not perform the cancellation itself.
- What was not tested: Other realtime providers, non-Twilio providers, and browser/UI talk transports were not live-tested in this proof.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Evidence is from one local macOS OpenClaw setup and one live phone call; sensitive call identifiers are redacted. Unit coverage in this PR exercises the provider VAD event path separately.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, the implementation cancelled from local MuLaw speech detection and did not cancel from provider `input_audio_buffer.speech_started`; this was verified by code inspection and covered by updating the existing barge-in unit test.

target.title: fix(hooks): honor hook-level model override for session-memory slug generation
target.body:
fix(hooks): honor hook-level model override for session-memory slug generation

## Summary
**Problem:** The session-memory hook's `llmSlug` feature always used the agent's default model (via `resolveDefaultModelForAgent`), ignoring the hook-level `model` config. Users who set `hooks.internal.entries.session-memory.model` to a specific model found that the slug generator still used the default model.

**Fix:** Add an optional `model` parameter to `generateSlugViaLLM`. When provided, use `splitModelRef` to properly parse provider-qualified model strings (e.g. `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`) instead of treating the entire string as a model-only ref. Pass `hookConfig?.model` from the session-memory handler.

**Out of scope:** No changes to model resolution logic, hook execution, or other hook types.

## Linked context
Closes #89551

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Session-memory hook's `model` config is ignored for LLM slug generation, always using the agent's default model
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node.js v22.11.0, OpenClaw latest main (commit dd0dd66, 2026-06-04)
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `splitModelRef` via `node --import tsx` to verify provider-qualified model parsing, and ran regression tests via vitest
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output showing the model parsing and tests passing:
```
$ node --import tsx -e "
import { splitModelRef } from './src/agents/subagent-spawn-plan.ts';

console.log('=== splitModelRef tests ===');

const result1 = splitModelRef('anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6');
console.log('anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 =>', JSON.stringify(result1));

const result2 = splitModelRef('gpt-5.5');
console.log('gpt-5.5 =>', JSON.stringify(result2));

const result3 = splitModelRef('deepseek/deepseek-v4');
console.log('deepseek/deepseek-v4 =>', JSON.stringify(result3));

const result4 = splitModelRef(undefined);
console.log('undefined =>', JSON.stringify(result4));
"

=== splitModelRef tests ===
anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 => {"provider":"anthropic","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}
gpt-5.5 => {"model":"gpt-5.5"}
deepseek/deepseek-v4 => {"provider":"deepseek","model":"deepseek-v4"}
undefined => {}

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts

 ✓  hooks  src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts
   ✓ generateSlugViaLLM
     ✓ keeps the helper default timeout when no agent timeout is configured
     ✓ marks the run lane-local so internal-helper failures do not poison shared profile health (#71709)
     ✓ honors configured agent timeoutSeconds for slow local providers
     ✓ infers provider metadata for bare configured agent models
     ✓ splits provider-qualified hook model override (regression #89551)
     ✓ uses hook model as model-only ref when no provider prefix (regression #89551)
     ✓ falls back to agent default when no hook model is provided (regression #89551)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)
```
- Observed result after fix: Provider-qualified model strings are properly split into provider and model parts. Model-only strings are treated as model-only refs. When no hook model is provided, the agent default is used.
- What was not tested: Live session-memory hook run with real LLM call (requires API credentials)

## Tests and validation
- `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts`: 3 new regression tests for provider-qualified override, model-only ref, and default fallback
- No CHANGELOG.md edits

## Risk checklist
- userVisible: No (internal hook behavior, no user-facing change)
- configEnvMigration: No
- securityAuthNetwork: No
- Mitigation: Only adds optional model parameter; backward compatible when not set

## Current review state
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1: Resolved hook model through existing provider/model parsing via splitModelRef
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2: Added focused regression tests for provider-qualified override and default fallback behavior
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1: Added real behavior proof showing splitModelRef correctly parses provider-qualified model strings
- [x] AI-assisted (built with Claude Code)
